== Overview == Kinomiya Faith is widely distributed along the {{ill|Sagami Bay|ja|相模灘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Bay|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相模湾|1=Sagami Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1061221}} coastal area, from western [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] to [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]'s [[:en:Izu Peninsula|Izu Peninsula]]. It primarily centers around shrines named "Kinomiya." The name Kinomiya is variously written as '''Kinomiya''' (木宮), '''Kinomiya''' (貴宮), '''Kinomiya''' (来宮), '''Kinomiya''' (黄宮), '''Kinomiya''' (木野宮), '''Kinomiya''' (紀伊宮), among others. The enshrined deities vary but frequently involve tree spirits or {{ill|driftwood deities|ja|漂着神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/driftwood deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its distribution overlaps significantly with areas known for the {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}} dance.
There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the name Kinomiya: *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (来の宮) Theory''' – Shrines situated along coasts, often founded around driftwood deities (gods that "arrived" from the sea). *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (木の宮) Theory''' – Derives from {{ill|tree worship|ja|樹木信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tree worship|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135540735}} or the faith of the [[:en:Kijiya|Kijishi woodworkers]]. *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (忌の宮) Theory''' – Based on {{ill|abstinence rituals|ja|忌み|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/abstinence rituals|1=Abstinence rituals|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5857008}} observed during ceremonies. *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (紀の宮) Theory''' – Related to gods from [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]], such as {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}, though "Kii" itself likely stems from the character for tree (木), thus possibly an extension of the "tree shrine" theory.
The lack of consensus is attributed to the faith's composite nature, involving aspects like driftwood shrines, tree worship, and unclear origins.
== Shrines of Kinomiya Faith == Notable shrines include: *'''{{ill|Kii Shrine|ja|紀伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Shrine|1=Kii Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112042855}}''' (Hayakawa, Odawara, Kanagawa) – Formerly Kii-no-Miya/Kinomiya Daigongen, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto and Prince Koretaka. *'''{{ill|Kibune Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|貴船神社 (真鶴町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibune Shrine (Manazuru)|1=Kifune Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634812}}''' (Manazuru, Ashigarashimo, Kanagawa) – Former district shrine known as Kimiyamiya Daimyōjin, enshrining Ōkuninushi, Kotoshironushi, and Sukunahikona. *'''{{ill|Kinomiya Shrine (Atami)|ja|来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinomiya Shrine (Atami)|1=Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523682}}''' (Nishiyama, Atami, Shizuoka) – Former Village shrine, currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Kinomiya Shrine (Izushi Hachiman)|ja|来宮神社 (伊豆市八幡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinomiya Shrine (Izushi Hachiman)|1=Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929624}}''' (Izushi, Shizuoka) – Former district shrine, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Hachimangu Kinomiya Shrine|ja|八幡宮来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimangu Kinomiya Shrine|1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390944}}''' (Hachimanno, Itō, Shizuoka) – Former district shrine, Kinomiya Shrine merged into a [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman Shrine]], enshrining Iwakura Wake-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Shiritaogi Shrine (Kinomiya Shrine)|ja|志理太乎宜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiritaogi Shrine (Kinomiya Shrine)|1=Shiritaogi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134892415}}''' (Shirata, Higashiizu, Shizuoka) – Former Village shrine, potential {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|ja|川津来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杉桙別命神社|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522170}} ({{ill|Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|杉桙別命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522170}})''' (Tanaka, Kawazu, Shizuoka) – Shikinaisha, former district shrine known as Kinomiya/Myōjin, enshrining Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto.
Additionally, many shrines within this area, while not explicitly named Kinomiya, also worship tree or driftwood deities and have unique abstinence rituals.
Historically, in the {{ill|Konan region of Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県#地域区分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konan region of Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131358}} within [[:en:Shiga District, Shiga|Shiga District, Ōmi Province]], a practice called '''Kinomiya Shōjin''' (季宮精進) occurred specifically on {{ill|November 23 (lunar calendar)|ja|11月23日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 23 (lunar calendar)|1=23rd day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840512}} to prevent fires by refraining from unnecessary fire usage and not consuming cooked food throughout the day. This practice is considered related to Kinomiya Faith<ref>『神道大辞典』, Heibonsha, 1937.</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == *{{ill|Folklore Research Institute|ja|民俗学研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folklore Research Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Minzokugaku Jiten'', {{ill|Tokyodo|ja|東京堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo|1=toukyoudou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524643}}, 1953 *{{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}} ed., ''Nihon no Kamigami: Jinja to Seichi'', Vol. 10 Tokai, {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|1=Hakusuisha Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346470}}, 1987
== See Also == *{{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}

Shrine|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522170}})''' (Tanaka, Kawazu, Shizuoka) – Shikinaisha, former district shrine known as Kinomiya/Myōjin, enshrining Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto.
Additionally, many shrines within this area, while not explicitly named Kinomiya, also worship tree or driftwood deities and have unique abstinence rituals.
Historically, in the {{ill|Konan region of Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県#地域区分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konan region of Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131358}} within [[:en:Shiga District, Shiga|Shiga District, Ōmi Province]], a practice called '''Kinomiya Shōjin''' (季宮精進) occurred specifically on {{ill|November 23 (lunar calendar)|ja|11月23日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 23 (lunar calendar)|1=23rd day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840512}} to prevent fires by refraining from unnecessary fire usage and not consuming cooked food throughout the day. This practice is considered related to Kinomiya Faith<ref>『神道大辞典』, Heibonsha, 1937.</ref>.

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:キノミヤ信仰]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:キノミヤ信仰]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:キノミヤ信仰]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11296898]] Wikidata name: Kinomiya faith [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11296898]] Wikidata name: Kinomiya faith [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kinomiya Faith''' (キノミヤ信仰) is a form of [[:en:Folk religion|Japanese folk religion]] found in [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
== Overview == Kinomiya Faith is widely distributed along the {{ill|Sagami Bay|ja|相模灘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Bay|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相模湾|1=Sagami Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1061221}} coastal area, from western [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] to [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]'s [[:en:Izu Peninsula|Izu Peninsula]]. It primarily centers around shrines named "Kinomiya." The name Kinomiya is variously written as '''Kinomiya''' (木宮), '''Kinomiya''' (貴宮), '''Kinomiya''' (来宮), '''Kinomiya''' (黄宮), '''Kinomiya''' (木野宮), '''Kinomiya''' (紀伊宮), among others. The enshrined deities vary but frequently involve tree spirits or {{ill|driftwood deities|ja|漂着神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/driftwood deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its distribution overlaps significantly with areas known for the {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}} dance.
There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the name Kinomiya: *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (来の宮) Theory''' – Shrines situated along coasts, often founded around driftwood deities (gods that "arrived" from the sea). *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (木の宮) Theory''' – Derives from {{ill|tree worship|ja|樹木信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tree worship|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135540735}} or the faith of the [[:en:Kijiya|Kijishi woodworkers]]. *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (忌の宮) Theory''' – Based on {{ill|abstinence rituals|ja|忌み|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/abstinence rituals|1=Abstinence rituals|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5857008}} observed during ceremonies. *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (紀の宮) Theory''' – Related to gods from [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]], such as {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}, though "Kii" itself likely stems from the character for tree (木), thus possibly an extension of the "tree shrine" theory.
The lack of consensus is attributed to the faith's composite nature, involving aspects like driftwood shrines, tree worship, and unclear origins.
== Shrines of Kinomiya Faith == Notable shrines include: *'''{{ill|Kii Shrine|ja|紀伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Shrine|1=Kii Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112042855}}''' (Hayakawa, Odawara, Kanagawa) – Formerly Kii-no-Miya/Kinomiya Daigongen, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto and Prince Koretaka. *'''{{ill|Kibune Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|貴船神社 (真鶴町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibune Shrine (Manazuru)|1=Kifune Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634812}}''' (Manazuru, Ashigarashimo, Kanagawa) – Former district shrine known as Kimiyamiya Daimyōjin, enshrining Ōkuninushi, Kotoshironushi, and Sukunahikona. *'''{{ill|Kinomiya Shrine (Atami)|ja|来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinomiya Shrine (Atami)|1=Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523682}}''' (Nishiyama, Atami, Shizuoka) – Former Village shrine, currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Kinomiya Shrine (Izushi Hachiman)|ja|来宮神社 (伊豆市八幡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinomiya Shrine (Izushi Hachiman)|1=Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929624}}''' (Izushi, Shizuoka) – Former district shrine, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Hachimangu Kinomiya Shrine|ja|八幡宮来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimangu Kinomiya Shrine|1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390944}}''' (Hachimanno, Itō, Shizuoka) – Former district shrine, Kinomiya Shrine merged into a [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman Shrine]], enshrining Iwakura Wake-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Shiritaogi Shrine (Kinomiya Shrine)|ja|志理太乎宜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiritaogi Shrine (Kinomiya Shrine)|1=Shiritaogi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134892415}}''' (Shirata, Higashiizu, Shizuoka) – Former Village shrine, potential {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|ja|川津来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杉桙別命神社|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522170}} ({{ill|Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|杉桙別命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522170}})''' (Tanaka, Kawazu, Shizuoka) – Shikinaisha, former district shrine known as Kinomiya/Myōjin, enshrining Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto.
Additionally, many shrines within this area, while not explicitly named Kinomiya, also worship tree or driftwood deities and have unique abstinence rituals.
Historically, in the {{ill|Konan region of Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県#地域区分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konan region of Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131358}} within [[:en:Shiga District, Shiga|Shiga District, Ōmi Province]], a practice called '''Kinomiya Shōjin''' (季宮精進) occurred specifically on {{ill|November 23 (lunar calendar)|ja|11月23日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 23 (lunar calendar)|1=23rd day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840512}} to prevent fires by refraining from unnecessary fire usage and not consuming cooked food throughout the day. This practice is considered related to Kinomiya Faith<ref>『神道大辞典』, Heibonsha, 1937.</ref>.

== Shrines of Kinomiya Faith == Notable shrines include: *'''{{ill|Kii Shrine|ja|紀伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Shrine|1=Kii Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112042855}}''' (Hayakawa, Odawara, Kanagawa) – Formerly Kii-no-Miya/Kinomiya Daigongen, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto and Prince Koretaka. *'''{{ill|Kibune Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|貴船神社 (真鶴町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibune Shrine (Manazuru)|1=Kifune Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634812}}''' (Manazuru, Ashigarashimo, Kanagawa) – Former district shrine known as Kimiyamiya Daimyōjin, enshrining Ōkuninushi, Kotoshironushi, and Sukunahikona. *'''{{ill|Kinomiya Shrine (Atami)|ja|来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinomiya Shrine (Atami)|1=Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523682}}''' (Nishiyama, Atami, Shizuoka) – Former Village shrine, currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Kinomiya Shrine (Izushi Hachiman)|ja|来宮神社 (伊豆市八幡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinomiya Shrine (Izushi Hachiman)|1=Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929624}}''' (Izushi, Shizuoka) – Former district shrine, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Hachimangu Kinomiya Shrine|ja|八幡宮来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimangu Kinomiya Shrine|1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390944}}''' (Hachimanno, Itō, Shizuoka) – Former district shrine, Kinomiya Shrine merged into a [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman Shrine]], enshrining Iwakura Wake-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Shiritaogi Shrine (Kinomiya Shrine)|ja|志理太乎宜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiritaogi Shrine (Kinomiya Shrine)|1=Shiritaogi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134892415}}''' (Shirata, Higashiizu, Shizuoka) – Former Village shrine, potential {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|ja|川津来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杉桙別命神社|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522170}} ({{ill|Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|杉桙別命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522170}})''' (Tanaka, Kawazu, Shizuoka) – Shikinaisha, former district shrine known as Kinomiya/Myōjin, enshrining Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto.
Additionally, many shrines within this area, while not explicitly named Kinomiya, also worship tree or driftwood deities and have unique abstinence rituals.
Historically, in the {{ill|Konan region of Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県#地域区分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konan region of Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131358}} within [[:en:Shiga District, Shiga|Shiga District, Ōmi Province]], a practice called '''Kinomiya Shōjin''' (季宮精進) occurred specifically on {{ill|November 23 (lunar calendar)|ja|11月23日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 23 (lunar calendar)|1=23rd day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840512}} to prevent fires by refraining from unnecessary fire usage and not consuming cooked food throughout the day. This practice is considered related to Kinomiya Faith<ref>『神道大辞典』, Heibonsha, 1937.</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:キノミヤ信仰]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:キノミヤ信仰]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:キノミヤ信仰]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11296898]] Wikidata name: Kinomiya faith [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11296898]] Wikidata name: Kinomiya faith [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kinomiya Faith''' (キノミヤ信仰) is a form of [[:en:Folk religion|Japanese folk religion]] found in [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
== Overview == Kinomiya Faith is widely distributed along the {{ill|Sagami Bay|ja|相模灘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Bay|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相模湾|1=Sagami Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1061221}} coastal area, from western [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] to [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]'s [[:en:Izu Peninsula|Izu Peninsula]]. It primarily centers around shrines named "Kinomiya." The name Kinomiya is variously written as '''Kinomiya''' (木宮), '''Kinomiya''' (貴宮), '''Kinomiya''' (来宮), '''Kinomiya''' (黄宮), '''Kinomiya''' (木野宮), '''Kinomiya''' (紀伊宮), among others. The enshrined deities vary but frequently involve tree spirits or {{ill|driftwood deities|ja|漂着神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/driftwood deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its distribution overlaps significantly with areas known for the {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}} dance.
There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the name Kinomiya: *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (来の宮) Theory''' – Shrines situated along coasts, often founded around driftwood deities (gods that "arrived" from the sea). *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (木の宮) Theory''' – Derives from {{ill|tree worship|ja|樹木信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tree worship|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135540735}} or the faith of the [[:en:Kijiya|Kijishi woodworkers]]. *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (忌の宮) Theory''' – Based on {{ill|abstinence rituals|ja|忌み|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/abstinence rituals|1=Abstinence rituals|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5857008}} observed during ceremonies. *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (紀の宮) Theory''' – Related to gods from [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]], such as {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}, though "Kii" itself likely stems from the character for tree (木), thus possibly an extension of the "tree shrine" theory.
The lack of consensus is attributed to the faith's composite nature, involving aspects like driftwood shrines, tree worship, and unclear origins.
== Shrines of Kinomiya Faith == Notable shrines include: *'''{{ill|Kii Shrine|ja|紀伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Shrine|1=Kii Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112042855}}''' (Hayakawa, Odawara, Kanagawa) – Formerly Kii-no-Miya/Kinomiya Daigongen, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto and Prince Koretaka. *'''{{ill|Kibune Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|貴船神社 (真鶴町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibune Shrine (Manazuru)|1=Kifune Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634812}}''' (Manazuru, Ashigarashimo, Kanagawa) – Former district shrine known as Kimiyamiya Daimyōjin, enshrining Ōkuninushi, Kotoshironushi, and Sukunahikona. *'''{{ill|Kinomiya Shrine (Atami)|ja|来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinomiya Shrine (Atami)|1=Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523682}}''' (Nishiyama, Atami, Shizuoka) – Former Village shrine, currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Kinomiya Shrine (Izushi Hachiman)|ja|来宮神社 (伊豆市八幡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinomiya Shrine (Izushi Hachiman)|1=Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929624}}''' (Izushi, Shizuoka) – Former district shrine, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Hachimangu Kinomiya Shrine|ja|八幡宮来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimangu Kinomiya Shrine|1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390944}}''' (Hachimanno, Itō, Shizuoka) – Former district shrine, Kinomiya Shrine merged into a [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman Shrine]], enshrining Iwakura Wake-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Shiritaogi Shrine (Kinomiya Shrine)|ja|志理太乎宜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiritaogi Shrine (Kinomiya Shrine)|1=Shiritaogi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134892415}}''' (Shirata, Higashiizu, Shizuoka) – Former Village shrine, potential {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki 杉桙別命神社|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522170}} ({{ill|Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|杉桙別命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522170}})''' (Tanaka, Kawazu, Shizuoka) – Shikinaisha, former district shrine known as Kinomiya/Myōjin, enshrining This practice is considered related to Kinomiya Faith<ref>『神道大辞典』, Heibonsha, 1937.</ref>.

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:キノミヤ信仰]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:キノミヤ信仰]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:キノミヤ信仰]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11296898]] Wikidata name: Kinomiya faith [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11296898]] Wikidata name: Kinomiya faith [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kinomiya Faith''' (キノミヤ信仰) is a form of {{ill|Folk religion|en|Folk religion|WD=Q1068640|lt=Japanese folk religion}} found in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.
== Overview == Kinomiya Faith is widely distributed along the {{ill|Sagami Bay|ja|相模灘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Bay|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相模湾|1=Sagami Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1061221}} coastal area, from western {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}} to {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}'s {{ill|Izu Peninsula|en|Izu Peninsula|WD=Q822822|lt=Izu Peninsula}}. It primarily centers around shrines named "Kinomiya." The name Kinomiya is variously written as '''Kinomiya''' (木宮), '''Kinomiya''' (貴宮), '''Kinomiya''' (来宮), '''Kinomiya''' (黄宮), '''Kinomiya''' (木野宮), '''Kinomiya''' (紀伊宮), among others. The enshrined deities vary but frequently involve tree spirits or {{ill|driftwood deities|ja|漂着神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/driftwood deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its distribution overlaps significantly with areas known for the {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}} dance.
There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the name Kinomiya: *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (来の宮) Theory''' – Shrines situated along coasts, often founded around driftwood deities (gods that "arrived" from the sea). *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (木の宮) Theory''' – Derives from {{ill|tree worship|ja|樹木信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tree worship|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135540735}} or the faith of the {{ill|Kijiya|en|Kijiya|WD=Q11518247|lt=Kijishi woodworkers}}. *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (忌の宮) Theory''' – Based on {{ill|abstinence rituals|ja|忌み|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/abstinence rituals|1=Abstinence rituals|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5857008}} observed during ceremonies. *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (紀の宮) Theory''' – Related to gods from {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province}}, such as {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}, though "Kii" itself likely stems from the character for tree (木), thus possibly an extension of the "tree shrine" theory.
The lack of consensus is attributed to the faith's composite nature, involving aspects like driftwood shrines, tree worship, and unclear origins.
== Shrines of Kinomiya Faith == Notable shrines include: *'''{{ill|Kii Shrine|ja|紀伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Shrine|1=Kii Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112042855}}''' (Hayakawa, Odawara, Kanagawa) – Formerly Kii-no-Miya/Kinomiya Daigongen, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto and Prince Koretaka. *'''{{ill|Kibune Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|貴船神社 (真鶴町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibune Shrine (Manazuru)|1=Kifune Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634812}}''' (Manazuru, Ashigarashimo, Kanagawa) – Former district shrine known as Kimiyamiya Daimyōjin, enshrining Ōkuninushi, Kotoshironushi, and Sukunahikona. *'''{{ill|Kinomiya Shrine (Atami)|ja|来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinomiya Shrine (Atami)|1=Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523682}}''' (Nishiyama, Atami, Shizuoka) – Former Village shrine, currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}}, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Kinomiya Shrine (Izushi Hachiman)|ja|来宮神社 (伊豆市八幡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinomiya Shrine (Izushi Hachiman)|1=Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929624}}''' (Izushi, Shizuoka) – Former district shrine, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Hachimangu Kinomiya Shrine|ja|八幡宮来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimangu Kinomiya Shrine|1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390944}}''' (Hachimanno, Itō, Shizuoka) – Former district shrine, Kinomiya Shrine merged into a {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman Shrine}}, enshrining Iwakura Wake-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Shiritaogi Shrine (Kinomiya Shrine)|ja|志理太乎宜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiritaogi Shrine (Kinomiya Shrine)|1=Shiritaogi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134892415}}''' (Shirata, Higashiizu, Shizuoka) – Former Village shrine, potential {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|ja|川津来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杉桙別命神社|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522170}} ({{ill|Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|杉桙別命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522170}})''' (Tanaka, Kawazu, Shizuoka) – Shikinaisha, former district shrine known as Kinomiya/Myōjin, enshrining Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto.
Additionally, many shrines within this area, while not explicitly named Kinomiya, also worship tree or driftwood deities and have unique abstinence rituals.
Historically, in the {{ill|Konan region of Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県#地域区分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konan region of Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131358}} within {{ill|Shiga District, Shiga|en|Shiga District, Shiga|WD=Q7496289|lt=Shiga District, Ōmi Province}}, a practice called '''Kinomiya Shōjin''' (季宮精進) occurred specifically on {{ill|November 23 (lunar calendar)|ja|11月23日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 23 (lunar calendar)|1=23rd day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840512}} to prevent fires by refraining from unnecessary fire usage and not consuming cooked food throughout the day. This practice is considered related to Kinomiya Faith<ref>『神道大辞典』, Heibonsha, 1937.</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == *{{ill|Folklore Research Institute|ja|民俗学研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folklore Research Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Minzokugaku Jiten'', {{ill|Tokyodo|ja|東京堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo|1=toukyoudou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524643}}, 1953 *{{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}} ed., ''Nihon no Kamigami: Jinja to Seichi'', Vol. 10 Tokai, {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|1=Hakusuisha Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346470}}, 1987
== See Also == *{{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}

enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Kinomiya Shrine (Izushi Hachiman)|ja|来宮神社 (伊豆市八幡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinomiya Shrine (Izushi Hachiman)|1=Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929624}}''' (Izushi, Shizuoka) – Former district shrine, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Hachimangu Kinomiya Shrine|ja|八幡宮来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimangu Kinomiya Shrine|1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390944}}''' (Hachimanno, Itō, Shizuoka) – Former district shrine, Kinomiya Shrine merged into a {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman Shrine}}, enshrining Iwakura Wake-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Shiritaogi Shrine (Kinomiya Shrine)|ja|志理太乎宜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiritaogi Shrine (Kinomiya Shrine)|1=Shiritaogi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134892415}}''' (Shirata, Higashiizu, Shizuoka) – Former Village shrine, potential {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|ja|川津来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杉桙別命神社|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522170}} ({{ill|Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|杉桙別命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522170}})''' (Tanaka, Kawazu, Shizuoka) – Shikinaisha, former district shrine known as Kinomiya/Myōjin, enshrining Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto.
Additionally, many shrines within this area, while not explicitly named Kinomiya, also worship tree or driftwood deities and have unique abstinence rituals.
Historically, in the {{ill|Konan region of Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県#地域区分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konan region of Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131358}} within {{ill|Shiga District, Shiga|en|Shiga District, Shiga|WD=Q7496289|lt=Shiga District, Ōmi Province}}, a practice called '''Kinomiya Shōjin''' (季宮精進) occurred specifically on {{ill|November 23 (lunar calendar)|ja|11月23日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 23 (lunar calendar)|1=23rd day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840512}} to prevent fires by refraining from unnecessary fire usage and not consuming cooked food throughout the day. This practice is considered related to Kinomiya Faith<ref>『神道大辞典』, Heibonsha, 1937.</ref>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:キノミヤ信仰]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:キノミヤ信仰]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:キノミヤ信仰]]
'''Kinomiya Faith''' (キノミヤ信仰) is a form of {{ill|Folk religion|en|Folk religion|WD=Q1068640|lt=Japanese folk religion}} found in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.
== Overview == Kinomiya Faith is widely distributed along the {{ill|Sagami Bay|ja|相模灘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Bay|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相模湾|1=Sagami Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1061221}} coastal area, from western {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}} to {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}'s {{ill|Izu Peninsula|en|Izu Peninsula|WD=Q822822|lt=Izu Peninsula}}. It primarily centers around shrines named "Kinomiya." The name Kinomiya is variously written as '''Kinomiya''' (木宮), '''Kinomiya''' (貴宮), '''Kinomiya''' (来宮), '''Kinomiya''' (黄宮), '''Kinomiya''' (木野宮), '''Kinomiya''' (紀伊宮), among others. The enshrined deities vary but frequently involve tree spirits or {{ill|driftwood deities|ja|漂着神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/driftwood deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its distribution overlaps significantly with areas known for the {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}} dance.
There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the name Kinomiya: *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (来の宮) Theory''' – Shrines situated along coasts, often founded around driftwood deities (gods that "arrived" from the sea). *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (木の宮) Theory''' – Derives from {{ill|tree worship|ja|樹木信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tree worship|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135540735}} or the faith of the {{ill|Kijiya|en|Kijiya|WD=Q11518247|lt=Kijishi woodworkers}}. *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (忌の宮) Theory''' – Based on {{ill|abstinence rituals|ja|忌み|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/abstinence rituals|1=Abstinence rituals|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5857008}} observed during ceremonies. *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (紀の宮) Theory''' – Related to gods from {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province}}, such as {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}, though "Kii" itself likely stems from the character for tree (木), thus possibly an extension of the "tree shrine" theory.
The lack of consensus is attributed to the faith's composite nature, involving aspects like driftwood shrines, tree worship, and unclear origins.
== Shrines of Kinomiya Faith == Notable shrines include: *'''{{ill|Kii Shrine|ja|紀伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Shrine|1=Kii Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112042855}}''' (Hayakawa, Odawara, Kanagawa) – Formerly Kii-no-Miya/Kinomiya Daigongen, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto and Prince Koretaka. *'''{{ill|Kibune Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|貴船神社 (真鶴町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibune Shrine (Manazuru)|1=Kifune Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634812}}''' (Manazuru, Ashigarashimo, Kanagawa) – Former district shrine known as Kimiyamiya Daimyōjin, enshrining Ōkuninushi, Kotoshironushi, and Sukunahikona. *'''{{ill|Kinomiya Shrine (Atami)|ja|来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinomiya Shrine (Atami)|1=Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523682}}''' (Nishiyama, Atami, Shizuoka) – Former Village shrine, currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}}, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Kinomiya Shrine (Izushi Hachiman)|ja|来宮神社 (伊豆市八幡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinomiya Shrine (Izushi Hachiman)|1=Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929624}}''' (Izushi, Shizuoka) – Former district shrine, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Hachimangu Kinomiya Shrine|ja|八幡宮来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimangu Kinomiya Shrine|1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390944}}''' (Hachimanno, Itō, Shizuoka) – Former district shrine, Kinomiya Shrine merged into a {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman Shrine}}, enshrining Iwakura Wake-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Shiritaogi Shrine (Kinomiya Shrine)|ja|志理太乎宜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiritaogi Shrine (Kinomiya Shrine)|1=Shiritaogi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134892415}}''' (Shirata, Higashiizu, Shizuoka) – Former Village shrine, potential {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|ja|川津来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杉桙別命神社|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522170}} ({{ill|Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|杉桙別命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522170}})''' (Tanaka, Kawazu, Shizuoka) – Shikinaisha, former district shrine known as Kinomiya/Myōjin, enshrining Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto.
Additionally, many shrines within this area, while not explicitly named Kinomiya, also worship tree or driftwood deities and have unique abstinence rituals.
Historically, in the {{ill|Konan region of Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県#地域区分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konan region of Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131358}} within {{ill|Shiga District, Shiga|en|Shiga District, Shiga|WD=Q7496289|lt=Shiga District, Ōmi Province}}, a practice called '''Kinomiya Shōjin''' (季宮精進) occurred specifically on {{ill|November 23 (lunar calendar)|ja|11月23日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 23 (lunar calendar)|1=23rd day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840512}} to prevent fires by refraining from unnecessary fire usage and not consuming cooked food throughout the day. This practice is considered related to Kinomiya Faith<ref>『神道大辞典』, Heibonsha, 1937.</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == *{{ill|Folklore Research Institute|ja|民俗学研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folklore Research Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Minzokugaku Jiten'', {{ill|Tokyodo|ja|東京堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo|1=toukyoudou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524643}}, 1953 *{{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}} ed., ''Nihon no Kamigami: Jinja to Seichi'', Vol. 10 Tokai, {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|1=Hakusuisha Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346470}}, 1987

'''Kinomiya Faith''' (キノミヤ信仰) is a form of {{ill|Folk religion|en|Folk religion|WD=Q1068640|lt=Japanese folk religion}} found in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.
== Overview == Kinomiya Faith is widely distributed along the {{ill|Sagami Bay|ja|相模灘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Bay|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相模湾|1=Sagami Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1061221}} coastal area, from western {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}} to {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}'s {{ill|Izu Peninsula|en|Izu Peninsula|WD=Q822822|lt=Izu Peninsula}}. It primarily centers around shrines named "Kinomiya." The name Kinomiya is variously written as '''Kinomiya''' (木宮), '''Kinomiya''' (貴宮), '''Kinomiya''' (来宮), '''Kinomiya''' (黄宮), '''Kinomiya''' (木野宮), '''Kinomiya''' (紀伊宮), among others. The enshrined deities vary but frequently involve tree spirits or {{ill|driftwood deities|ja|漂着神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/driftwood deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its distribution overlaps significantly with areas known for the {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}} dance.
There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the name Kinomiya: *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (来の宮) Theory''' – Shrines situated along coasts, often founded around driftwood deities (gods that "arrived" from the sea). *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (木の宮) Theory''' – Derives from {{ill|tree worship|ja|樹木信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tree worship|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135540735}} or the faith of the {{ill|Kijiya|en|Kijiya|WD=Q11518247|lt=Kijishi woodworkers}}. *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (忌の宮) Theory''' – Based on {{ill|abstinence rituals|ja|忌み|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/abstinence rituals|1=Abstinence rituals|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5857008}} observed during ceremonies. *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (紀の宮) Theory''' – Related to gods from {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province}}, such as {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}, though "Kii" itself likely stems from the character for tree (木), thus possibly an extension of the "tree shrine" theory.
The lack of consensus is attributed to the faith's composite nature, involving aspects like driftwood shrines, tree worship, and unclear origins.
== Shrines of Kinomiya Faith == Notable shrines include: *'''{{ill|Kii Shrine|ja|紀伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Shrine|1=Kii Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112042855}}''' (Hayakawa, Odawara, Kanagawa) – Formerly Kii-no-Miya/Kinomiya Daigongen, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto and Prince Koretaka. *'''{{ill|Kibune Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|貴船神社 (真鶴町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibune Shrine (Manazuru)|1=Kifune Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634812}}''' (Manazuru, Ashigarashimo, Kanagawa) – Former district shrine known as Kimiyamiya Daimyōjin, enshrining Ōkuninushi, Kotoshironushi, and Sukunahikona. *'''{{ill|Kinomiya Shrine (Atami)|ja|来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinomiya Shrine (Atami)|1=Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523682}}''' (Nishiyama, Atami, Shizuoka) – Former Village shrine, currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}}, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Kinomiya Shrine (Izushi Hachiman)|ja|来宮神社 (伊豆市八幡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinomiya Shrine (Izushi Hachiman)|1=Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929624}}''' (Izushi, Shizuoka) – Former district shrine, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Hachimangu Kinomiya Shrine|ja|八幡宮来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimangu Kinomiya Shrine|1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390944}}''' (Hachimanno, Itō, Shizuoka) – Former district shrine, Kinomiya Shrine merged into a {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman Shrine}}, enshrining Iwakura Wake-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Shiritaogi Shrine (Kinomiya Shrine)|ja|志理太乎宜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiritaogi Shrine (Kinomiya Shrine)|1=Shiritaogi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134892415}}''' (Shirata, Higashiizu, Shizuoka) – Former Village shrine, potential {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|ja|川津来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杉桙別命神社|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522170}} ({{ill|Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|杉桙別命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522170}})''' (Tanaka, Kawazu, Shizuoka) – Shikinaisha, former district shrine known as Kinomiya/Myōjin, enshrining Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto.
Additionally, many shrines within this area, while not explicitly named Kinomiya, also worship tree or driftwood deities and have unique abstinence rituals.
Historically, in the {{ill|Konan region of Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県#地域区分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konan region of Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131358}} within {{ill|Shiga District, Shiga|en|Shiga District, Shiga|WD=Q7496289|lt=Shiga District, Ōmi Province}}, a practice called '''Kinomiya Shōjin''' (季宮精進) occurred specifically on {{ill|November 23 (lunar calendar)|ja|11月23日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 23 (lunar calendar)|1=23rd day of the 11th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840512}} to prevent fires by refraining from unnecessary fire usage and not consuming cooked food throughout the day. This practice is considered related to Kinomiya Faith<ref>『神道大辞典』, Heibonsha, 1937.</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == *{{ill|Folklore Research Institute|ja|民俗学研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folklore Research Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Minzokugaku Jiten'', {{ill|Tokyodo|ja|東京堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo|1=toukyoudou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524643}}, 1953 *{{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}} ed., ''Nihon no Kamigami: Jinja to Seichi'', Vol. 10 Tokai, {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|1=Hakusuisha Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346470}}, 1987
== See Also == *{{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}

Shrine|ja|杉桙別命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522170}})''' (Tanaka, Kawazu, Shizuoka) – Shikinaisha, former district shrine known as Kinomiya/Myōjin, enshrining Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto.
Additionally, many shrines within this area, while not explicitly named Kinomiya, also worship tree or driftwood deities and have unique abstinence rituals.
Historically, in the {{ill|Konan region of Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県#地域区分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konan region of Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131358}} within {{ill|Shiga District, Shiga|en|Shiga District, Shiga|WD=Q7496289|lt=Shiga District, Ōmi Province|1=Shiga District, Shiga}}, a practice called '''Kinomiya Shōjin''' (季宮精進) occurred specifically on {{ill|November 23 (lunar calendar)|ja|11月23日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 23 (lunar calendar)|1=November 23 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840512}} to prevent fires by refraining from unnecessary fire usage and not consuming cooked food throughout the day. This practice is considered related to Kinomiya Faith<ref>『神道大辞典』, Heibonsha, 1937.</ref>.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:キノミヤ信仰]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:キノミヤ信仰]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:キノミヤ信仰]]
'''Kinomiya Faith''' (キノミヤ信仰) is a form of {{ill|Folk religion|en|Folk religion|WD=Q1068640|lt=Japanese folk religion|1=Folk religion}} found in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.
== Overview == Kinomiya Faith is widely distributed along the {{ill|Sagami Bay|ja|相模灘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Bay|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相模湾|1=Sagami Bay|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1061221}} coastal area, from western {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}} to {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}'s {{ill|Izu Peninsula|en|Izu Peninsula|WD=Q822822|lt=Izu Peninsula|1=Izu Peninsula}}. It primarily centers around shrines named "Kinomiya." The name Kinomiya is variously written as '''Kinomiya''' (木宮), '''Kinomiya''' (貴宮), '''Kinomiya''' (来宮), '''Kinomiya''' (黄宮), '''Kinomiya''' (木野宮), '''Kinomiya''' (紀伊宮), among others. The enshrined deities vary but frequently involve tree spirits or {{ill|driftwood deities|ja|漂着神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/driftwood deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its distribution overlaps significantly with areas known for the {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}} dance.
There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the name Kinomiya: *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (来の宮) Theory''' – Shrines situated along coasts, often founded around driftwood deities (gods that "arrived" from the sea). *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (木の宮) Theory''' – Derives from {{ill|tree worship|ja|樹木信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tree worship|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135540735|1=Q135540735}} or the faith of the {{ill|Kijiya|en|Kijiya|WD=Q11518247|lt=Kijishi woodworkers|1=Kijiya}}. *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (忌の宮) Theory''' – Based on {{ill|abstinence rituals|ja|忌み|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/abstinence rituals|1=Abstinence rituals|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5857008}} observed during ceremonies. *'''"Ki-no-Miya" (紀の宮) Theory''' – Related to gods from {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}, such as {{ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}, though "Kii" itself likely stems from the character for tree (木), thus possibly an extension of the "tree shrine" theory.
The lack of consensus is attributed to the faith's composite nature, involving aspects like driftwood shrines, tree worship, and unclear origins.
== Shrines of Kinomiya Faith == Notable shrines include: *'''{{ill|Kii Shrine|ja|紀伊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Shrine|1=Kii Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q112042855}}''' (Hayakawa, Odawara, Kanagawa) – Formerly Kii-no-Miya/Kinomiya Daigongen, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto and Prince Koretaka. *'''{{ill|Kibune Shrine (Manazuru)|ja|貴船神社 (真鶴町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibune Shrine (Manazuru)|1=Kifune Shrine (Manazuru, Kanagawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11634812}}''' (Manazuru, Ashigarashimo, Kanagawa) – Former district shrine known as Kimiyamiya Daimyōjin, enshrining Ōkuninushi, Kotoshironushi, and Sukunahikona. *'''{{ill|Kinomiya Shrine (Atami)|ja|来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinomiya Shrine (Atami)|1=Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523682}}''' (Nishiyama, Atami, Shizuoka) – Former Village shrine, currently a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}}, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Kinomiya Shrine (Izushi Hachiman)|ja|来宮神社 (伊豆市八幡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinomiya Shrine (Izushi Hachiman)|1=Kinomiya Shrine (Q119929624)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929624}}''' (Izushi, Shizuoka) – Former district shrine, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Hachimangu Kinomiya Shrine|ja|八幡宮来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimangu Kinomiya Shrine|1=Hachimangū Kinomiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11390944}}''' (Hachimanno, Itō, Shizuoka) – Former district shrine, Kinomiya Shrine merged into a {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman}}, enshrining Iwakura Wake-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Shiritaogi Shrine (Kinomiya Shrine)|ja|志理太乎宜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiritaogi Shrine (Kinomiya Shrine)|1=Shiritaogi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134892415}}''' (Shirata, Higashiizu, Shizuoka) – Former Village shrine, potential {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, enshrining Itakeru-no-Mikoto. *'''{{ill|Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|ja|川津来宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawazu Kinomiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杉桙別命神社|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522170}} ({{ill|Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|杉桙別命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto Shrine|1=Sugihokowake no Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522170}})''' (Tanaka, Kawazu, Shizuoka) – Shikinaisha, former district shrine known as Kinomiya/Myōjin, enshrining Sugihokowake-no-Mikoto.
Additionally, many shrines within this area, while not explicitly named Kinomiya, also worship tree or driftwood deities and have unique abstinence rituals.
Historically, in the {{ill|Konan region of Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県#地域区分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konan region of Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131358}} within {{ill|Shiga District, Shiga|en|Shiga District, Shiga|WD=Q7496289|lt=Shiga District, Ōmi Province|1=Shiga District, Shiga}}, a practice called '''Kinomiya Shōjin''' (季宮精進) occurred specifically on {{ill|November 23 (lunar calendar)|ja|11月23日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/November 23 (lunar calendar)|1=November 23 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q840512}} to prevent fires by refraining from unnecessary fire usage and not consuming cooked food throughout the day. This practice is considered related to Kinomiya Faith<ref>『神道大辞典』, Heibonsha, 1937.</ref>.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == *{{ill|Folklore Research Institute|ja|民俗学研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folklore Research Institute|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Minzokugaku Jiten'', {{ill|Tokyodo|ja|東京堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo|1=toukyoudou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524643}}, 1953 *{{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}} ed., ''Nihon no Kamigami: Jinja to Seichi'', Vol. 10 Tokai, {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|1=Hakusuisha Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346470}}, 1987
== See Also == *{{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}}
